title: Die Kildare-Gedichte (U-M main-text-and-appendix selection)
summary: Evidence-separated lineage for the bounded U-M selection of Heuser's title page, seventeen-poem main text, and scholarly appendix. The graph distinguishes the pinned repository XML, conceptual/current U-M item, exact raw U-M XML member, raw compound carrier, 1904 scholarly edition, historical intermediary editions, nineteen manuscript carriers, modern support expressions, and provider-specific facsimile, access, and rights layers. Direct and indirect witness routes remain explicit, and the disputed Harley 1706 attribution is retained only as tentative.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is an edited selection, not a single poem, authorial collection, complete codex, or complete 1904 volume.
Only the first poem is assigned to Friar Michael Kildare; the remaining main poems and the whole selection have no blanket medieval author.
Complete IIIF coverage means complete provider-exposed canvas sequence unless physical-exterior completeness is explicitly proven.

entities.label: General Corpus pinned XML for ajt2514
entities.description: Repository-normalized XML for the bounded title-page, main-text, and appendix selection.
notes: The CME submodule commit pins this file; copied_from denotes normalized textual and structural descent, not byte identity.

entities.label: University of Michigan conceptual/current AJT2514.0001.001 item
entities.description: The conceptual/current U-M electronic item addressed by the name resolver, kept distinct from the 2011 Internet Archive raw package.
notes: Scripted checking reached a challenge-protected endpoint; this does not show that the item is gone or that current bytes equal the raw package.

entities.label: Raw U-M AJT2514.0001.001 XML member
entities.description: Exact embedded XML member ajt2514.0001.001.xml in the raw U-M package, containing the selected title page, main text, and appendix.
entities.reproduction_format: SGML-derived XML member inside Raw Michigan Book ZIP
notes: Member size 323,588 bytes; SHA-256 3b1a577a7635300ac5eb543661aa86b1cb965b1abab49e0817144e7fc6dfcc84.
The raw carrier access and exact member locator provide access; the ZIP local-copy record is not duplicated here.

entities.label: Wilhelm Heuser, Die Kildare-Gedichte (1904)
entities.description: Complete 1904 scholarly edition from which U-M selected the title page, main text on pages 77-184, and appendix on pages 185-229.

bibliographic.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, Die Kildare-Gedichte: Die ältesten mittelenglischen Denkmäler in anglo-irischer Überlieferung. Bonner Beiträge zur Anglistik 14. Bonn: P. Hanstein, 1904.
bibliographic.title: Die Kildare-Gedichte: Die ältesten mittelenglischen Denkmäler in anglo-irischer Überlieferung

notes: The lineage graph scopes every witness relation to selected passages; it does not treat the complete volume as the cataloging subject.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Heuser 1904
entities.description: Complete 254-page Harvard/Google-source scan with PDF, OCR, layout OCR, metadata, and scandata deliveries.
entities.reproduction_format: Whole-book scan, PDF, OCR, layout OCR, and scan metadata

entities.label: HathiTrust record 100324504 for Heuser 1904
entities.description: Bibliographic and item-rights record for the Harvard copy that identifies full-view pdus access in the United States.

entities.label: Internet Archive raw U-M AJT2514 umichbook carrier
entities.description: Compound Raw Michigan Book ZIP with 240 complete-volume TIFFs, exact raw XML, pageview and header control files, a tagged text-body fragment, and load log.
entities.reproduction_format: Raw Michigan Book ZIP: 240 Group-4 TIFFs, XML, text, header, pageview, and load log
notes: All 240 TIFF MD5 values and XML page references were verified; image 1 is the title page, 85 begins printed p. 77, 193 begins p. 185, and 240 is the colophon.

entities.label: U-M AJT2514 coding instructions
entities.description: Book-specific instruction page defining the title/main/appendix electronic selection and division hierarchy.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 913
entities.description: Principal source of all seventeen main poem components: a mixed trilingual parchment miscellany from south-east Ireland, probably Waterford.

holding.notes: 64 folios plus flyleaves; main components occur at discontiguous loci.

notes: Current catalog favors south-east Ireland, probably Waterford; Kildare remains a conventional label.
Only the hymn at ff. 9r-10r names Friar Michael Kildare.

entities.label: British Library complete IIIF facsimile of Harley MS 913
entities.description: Complete 145-canvas provider sequence containing every main-text target locus.
notes: Complete means the complete provider-exposed canvas sequence; no stronger claim about every physical exterior surface or archival master is made.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Cleopatra C VI
entities.description: Carrier of the seven-line comparison version for main component 17 at Heuser's f. 22.
notes: Heuser says nach der Handschrift; current parent/component records encompass f. 22 but do not itemize this short entry.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Rawlinson poet. 32
entities.description: Carrier of the six-line analogue at f. 1v, mediated to Heuser through Wright and Halliwell rather than directly transcribed by Heuser.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian published selection for Rawlinson poet. 32
entities.description: The complete two-canvas published selection, ff. 30r and 57r, with zero overlap with the target at f. 1v.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 463
entities.description: Late-fourteenth-century parchment carrier of appendix Commandments and Seven Sins versions at Heuser ff. 157r-159v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian published selection for Laud Misc. 463
entities.description: One published image, f. 115r, which does not overlap the target at ff. 157r-159v.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1706
entities.description: Historically attributed by Heuser as the source of appendix Decem Mandata and Seven Sins selections at fol. 306; the present record conflicts in support, extent, and contents.

physical_description.extent: Current catalog: 216 folios; Heuser prints fol. 306
notes: The historical/current contradiction remains unresolved; Harley 5396 is not substituted.

entities.label: British Library Arundel MS 20
entities.description: Fifteenth-century paper carrier of two seven-line appendix stanzas related to Commandments and Seven Sins tracts.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 416
entities.description: Fifteenth-century paper carrier of an appendix Seven Sins opening at Heuser f. 35.

notes: Dated in part 1459.

entities.label: New College, Oxford, MS 88
entities.description: Carrier of the fifteen-line Christ-on-the-Cross analogue at f. 179r-v.

entities.label: H. O. Coxe, Catalogus codicum MSS. ... Oxoniensibus, Part I (1852)
entities.description: Historical Oxford college-manuscript catalog supporting New College MS 88 and its locus.

bibliographic.citation: Henry O. Coxe, Catalogus codicum MSS. qui in collegiis aulisque Oxoniensibus hodie adservantur, Part I. Oxford, 1852.
bibliographic.title: Catalogus codicum MSS. qui in collegiis aulisque Oxoniensibus hodie adservantur

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodl. 42
entities.description: Early-fourteenth-century parchment carrier of two poems at f. 250; Heuser directly checked the ten-line Christ analogue.

physical_description.support: Parchment in the relevant early-fourteenth-century part

entities.label: Durham Cathedral Library MS A.III.12
entities.description: Carrier of a four-line Christ-on-the-Cross analogue at f. 49r, mediated to Heuser through Furnivall.

physical_description.extent: Middle English fourth item at f. 49r

entities.label: Durham Priory Library Project complete IIIF facsimile of A.III.12
entities.description: Complete 460-canvas provider sequence; the target at f. 49r is canvas 101.
notes: Complete means the complete provider-exposed sequence, not a verified inventory of every physical exterior surface or archival master.

entities.label: British Library Arundel MS 507
entities.description: Composite paper/parchment carrier whose fourteenth-century component includes the exact eight-line Christ analogue at f. 10r.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 31042
entities.description: Carrier of the sixteen-line Christ analogue at Heuser f. 94v/current f. 94; Heuser's parchment description conflicts with the current chiefly paper description.

physical_description.support: Current catalog: paper except some leaves; Heuser: parchment
physical_description.extent: Target at current f. 94 / Heuser f. 94v
notes: Both physical and locator claims are retained without premature harmonization.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 7358
entities.description: Paper carrier of the thirty-six-line Lullay at f. 12v; current catalog dates the manuscript to the fifteenth century while Heuser assigned the fourteenth.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ii.4.9
entities.description: Fifteenth-century, 197-leaf paper manuscript with the eighty-two-line Earth version at ff. 67r-68v.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 598
entities.description: Carrier of the Conquest of Ireland prose extract at ff. 14v-15r.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library complete published sequence for MS 598
entities.description: All 310 currently published, numerically labeled LUNA canvases; target ff. 14v-15r are canvases 47-48.
notes: The audit did not prove inclusion of every board, cover, spine, pastedown, or other exterior surface.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 633
entities.description: Late-fifteenth-century parchment carrier of the Secreta Secretorum prose extract at ff. 45r-46v.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library published image selections for MS 633
entities.description: Public images for f. 84r and f. 85v only; neither overlaps the target at ff. 45r-46v.
entities.reproduction_format: Two separate one-canvas LUNA IIIF objects

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 623
entities.description: Sixteenth-century Carew/Book of Howth compilation carrying the Conquest of Ireland werewolf prose extract at ff. 47r-v.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library published image selection for MS 623
entities.description: Seven published images, nearest target-adjacent image f. 48v; target ff. 47r-v are absent.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 526
entities.description: Mixed parchment/paper carrier of a Conquest of Ireland prose extract on the final parchment leaf.

notes: Current TEI describes mid-fifteenth-century Irish annals but does not itemize the target.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 104
entities.description: One physical witness supplying Tutivillus at f. 112v and four Piers Plowman C-text extracts at ff. 12r-v, 13v-14v, 35r-v, and 67r-v.

physical_description.extent: 112 folios plus inserted 33a/33b leaves in the published sequence

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete published folio sequence for Douce 104
entities.description: All 226 provider canvases from fol. 1r through fol. 112v, including inserted 33a and 33b; all five target loci are present.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest, 226 published folio canvases
notes: Complete provider-exposed folio sequence, not complete physical-object coverage or an archival master; no covers, boards, spine, or pastedowns are in the provider sequence.

entities.label: Furnivall, Political, Religious, and Love Poems (EETS 15, 1866)
entities.description: Immediate printed source for Heuser's four-line Durham A.III.12 excerpt and a comparative source for Bodl. 42.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Political, Religious, and Love Poems. EETS Original Series 15. London, 1866.
bibliographic.title: Political, Religious, and Love Poems
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 15

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Furnivall EETS 15
entities.description: Complete 338-page PDF and OCR deliveries; printed p. 214 / PDF p. 274 / DjVu leaf 273 carries the Durham and Bodl. 42 four-line versions.
entities.reproduction_format: Whole-book PDF, OCR, layout OCR, metadata, and scandata

entities.label: Wright and Halliwell, Reliquiae Antiquae, volume I (1845)
entities.description: Immediate source for Heuser's Rawlinson analogue and comparator for doubtful bracketed Tutivillus restorations.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, eds., Reliquiae Antiquae, volume I. London, 1845.
bibliographic.title: Reliquiae Antiquae: Scraps from Ancient Manuscripts

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Reliquiae Antiquae volume I
entities.description: Complete 342-page PDF and OCR deliveries; printed p. 257 carries Tutivillus and p. 316 the Rawlinson analogue.
entities.reproduction_format: Whole-book PDF, OCR, metadata, and scandata

entities.label: Skeat, The Vision of William concerning Piers the Plowman, Part III (EETS 54, 1873)
entities.description: Comparator for the four Piers Plowman C-text passages transcribed directly by Heuser from Douce 104.

bibliographic.citation: Walter W. Skeat, ed., The Vision of William concerning Piers the Plowman, Part III. EETS Original Series 54. London, 1873.
bibliographic.title: The Vision of William concerning Piers the Plowman, Part III

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Skeat EETS 54
entities.description: Complete 680-page PDF and OCR deliveries; comparison passages are at printed pp. 46, 52, 139, and 264.
entities.reproduction_format: Whole-book PDF, OCR, metadata, and scandata

entities.label: Angela Lucas, The Kildare Poems (UCC CELT E300000-001)
entities.description: Modern complete edition of seventeen independently headed main-poem counterparts, including the seven-line Five Hateful Things; it does not contain Heuser's appendix.
notes: Used as independent structural support, not asserted as a historical source for Heuser.

entities.label: Lucía Sinisi, Urbanization and Pollution in an Irish (?) Town in the 14th Century
entities.description: Secondary support study explaining that a conventional count of sixteen becomes seventeen when Five Hateful Things at f. 6v is included.

bibliographic.citation: Lucía Sinisi, Urbanization and Pollution in an Irish (?) Town in the 14th Century, Selim 12 (2003-2004): 160-178.
bibliographic.title: Urbanization and Pollution in an Irish (?) Town in the 14th Century

entities.description: Archived official page supporting the item-linked public-domain image assertion, attribution request, and no-new-restrictions request; the live page returns 404.

U-M AJT2514 coding instructions

rights.component: Pinned XML serialization and embedded source content
rights.basis: The repository top-level Unlicense expressly covers software and does not by itself settle rights in embedded source XML, U-M encoding work, Heuser editorial apparatus, or provider-supplied content.
notes: Medieval textual content and the 1904 edition layer have separate public-domain records.

rights.component: Conceptual/current U-M electronic presentation and encoding layer
rights.basis: Current U-M policy leaves public-domain evaluation to users and requests attribution; the challenge-protected endpoint did not expose item-specific content-layer terms during scripted checking.
notes: The raw package's missing license is not imported as a current U-M rights statement.

rights.component: Raw U-M XML serialization and encoding layer
rights.basis: The exact embedded legacy notice states that copyright applies where applicable and that other distribution restrictions may apply.
notes: The medieval text and 1904 source edition are separate public-domain layers.

rights.basis: Published in 1904; HathiTrust marks the Harvard item pdus and full view in the United States.

rights.component: Underlying 1904 edition in Internet Archive scan and OCR deliveries
rights.basis: The underlying 1904 edition is public domain in the United States; delivery-platform terms remain distinct.

rights.component: HathiTrust catalog metadata and access assertions
rights.basis: No separate open license for the downloaded catalog JSON was established; its pdus code applies to the underlying item, not automatically to catalog metadata.

rights.component: Raw U-M TIFF digitization and compound ZIP packaging layer
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata supplies no license for the raw digitization or package layer; the underlying 1904 edition is separately public domain.
notes: Do not infer an open license from public download access.

rights.basis: No item-level license is displayed on the cached instruction page.
Medieval textual content is public domain by age in the United States; this does not determine image or access terms.

rights.component: British Library Harley MS 913 unpublished-manuscript images
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest points to a now-404 live reuse page; the archived official page states that designated unpublished-manuscript images were released into the public domain.
notes: Credit requested: The British Library, shelfmark and folio. The archived page also asks downstream users not to impose new restrictions; these are requests, not a permission requirement.

rights.basis: Medieval textual content is public domain by age; modern photography and access terms are separate.

editorial_practices.representation: Title page, main text pp. 77-184, and appendix pp. 185-229
editorial_practices.description: U-M deliberately encoded image 1 for the title page, images 85-192 for the main text, and images 193-237 for the appendix, omitting the introduction and remaining book matter.
Heuser selected seventeen principal Harley poem components, nested translations and analogues, and appendix extracts from larger manuscript carriers.
notes: The selected appendix publications remain scholarly apparatus, not newly counted intellectual works.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript abbreviations including a', i-c, h, and k
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded editorial forms, including Iesus and Iesu
editorial_practices.description: Heuser states that manuscript abbreviations are expanded according to his editorial conventions.
editorial_practices.scope: Transcribed medieval texts in the selected main text and appendix

editorial_practices.source_feature: Earlier English editions and direct manuscript readings
editorial_practices.representation: Variants, restorations, comparison passages, and readings Heuser believed earlier editors modernized or misread
editorial_practices.description: Heuser collated against earlier editions while retaining direct manuscript sources where documented; the Durham and Rawlinson intermediary routes and the Douce comparator routes remain explicit in the graph.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected main analogues and appendix comparison passages

editorial_practices.source_feature: Red manuscript heading for Quindecim Signa
editorial_practices.description: The source rubric is represented editorially rather than as a new textual item.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME XML for ajt2514.
evidence.locator: HEADER, TITLEPAGE, main DIV1, appendix DIV1, and all descendants
evidence.summary: Confirms the repository artifact, electronic identifier, 2006 U-M responsibility, 1904 source edition, uncorrected OCR declaration, selected hierarchy, 240 page references, and exact text structure.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive raw U-M AJT2514 umichbook package and exact member audit.
evidence.locator: 245 members: 240 TIFFs plus XML, TXT, HDR, pageview.dat, and loadcd.log
evidence.summary: Verifies package fixity, 240 continuous page images, all TIFF MD5 values, exact raw XML member identity, page-reference equality, boundary images, and normalization-only differences between raw and pinned XML. The ZIP is cited once through its carrier access.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan AJT2514 coding instructions.
evidence.summary: Explicitly orders the title page at image 1, main text pp. 77-184 at images 85-192, and appendix pp. 185-229 at images 193-237, and distinguishes front/title, texts, and appendix hierarchy.

evidence.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, Die Kildare-Gedichte (Bonn, 1904), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: Title page; main text pp. 77-184; appendix pp. 185-229; editorial introductions and source notes
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the title, editor, seventeen main components, exact printed loci, appendix structure and quantities, named Michael attribution, manuscript claims, direct/indirect source statements, disputed Harley 1706 fol. 306, and editorial practices.

evidence.citation: HathiTrust bibliographic and item record 100324504 for Heuser 1904.
evidence.summary: Confirms the Harvard copy, bibliographic identity, Hathi item hvd.32044011402047, US full view, and pdus rights code.

evidence.citation: British Library catalog and complete IIIF object for Harley MS 913.
evidence.locator: 145 canvases; all exact main-text loci from ff. 3r through 63v, discontiguous
evidence.summary: Supports the current date, parchment support, south-east Irish probable Waterford origin, mixed contents, itemization of Five Evil Things at f. 6v, exact main loci, complete provider sequence, and direct visual verification.

evidence.citation: Official British Library and Bodleian catalog records for the main analogue and appendix witnesses.
evidence.summary: Supports current carrier identities, shelfmarks, support/date/extent where stated, exact Arundel and Harley itemization, and target-image gaps. Catalog descriptions are support evidence, not target transcriptions.

evidence.citation: British Library current parent and component records for Harley MS 1706, compared with Heuser's printed attribution.
evidence.locator: Current 216-folio parchment extent and ff. 3r-95v component record; Heuser appendix pp. 205-206 and fol. 306
evidence.summary: The current record gives parchment, 216 folios, and nonmatching contents, while Heuser says paper and prints fol. 306. The contradiction is preserved and the relation remains tentative; Harley 5396 is not substituted.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF objects and image attribution for Douce 104, Laud Misc. 463, and Rawlinson poet. 32.
evidence.summary: Supports exact canvas inventories, target presence or absence, provider-sequence boundaries, and the Bodleian CC BY-NC 4.0 photography attribution.

evidence.citation: Bodleian MS Douce 104 catalog and complete 226-canvas provider sequence.
evidence.summary: Confirms that all five appendix extracts belong to one physical witness and are visible in the complete provider-exposed folio sequence. The sequence excludes physical exteriors.
notes: A transient buffered-page-cache mismatch was resolved by cache eviction without modifying or replacing the inode; subsequent ordinary/direct hashes and full ZIP tests agree at this recorded hash.

evidence.citation: Durham Priory Library Project catalog and complete IIIF object for Durham Cathedral Library MS A.III.12.
evidence.summary: Confirms the Middle English fourth item at f. 49r, target image presence, complete provider sequence, and the manifest's CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 license and attribution.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library repository copy of M. R. James's catalog description for MS Ii.4.9.
evidence.summary: Supports the CUL shelfmark, fifteenth-century date, paper support, 197-leaf extent, target locus, repository access route, and CC BY 4.0 license for the catalog PDF only.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library LUNA search records, IIIF objects, registration page, and imaging-service terms for MSS 598, 623, and 633.
evidence.locator: MS 598 complete 310-canvas sequence and target canvases 47-48; MS 623/633 target-omitting selections
evidence.summary: Confirms MS 598 target presence and provider-sequence coverage, MS 623 and 633 target absence from current selections, and provider Rights: LPL / permission-required publication terms.

evidence.citation: Henry O. Coxe, Catalogus codicum MSS. ... Oxoniensibus, Part I (1852), Internet Archive OCR.
evidence.summary: Supports the historical New College carrier description and appointment-based access route; no complete public target facsimile was verified.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, Political, Religious, and Love Poems, EETS 15 (1866), complete scan and OCR.
evidence.locator: Printed p. 214 / PDF p. 274 / DjVu leaf 273
evidence.summary: Confirms bibliographic identity and the four-line Durham A.III.12 and Bodl. 42 versions on the exact intermediary page used by Heuser for Durham.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, Reliquiae Antiquae, volume I (1845), complete scan and OCR.
evidence.locator: Tutivillus at printed p. 257 / PDF p. 267; Rawlinson analogue at p. 316 / PDF p. 326
evidence.summary: Confirms bibliographic identity, Heuser's immediate source for the Rawlinson analogue, and the comparator used for doubtful Tutivillus restorations.

evidence.citation: Walter W. Skeat, The Vision of William concerning Piers the Plowman, Part III, EETS 54 (1873), complete scan and OCR.
evidence.summary: Confirms bibliographic identity and exact comparison passages; Skeat remains a comparator, not the direct manuscript source.

evidence.citation: Angela Lucas, The Kildare Poems, UCC CELT E300000-001.
evidence.locator: Seventeen independently headed poems; Poem 2, Five Hateful Things
evidence.summary: Independently supports a seventeen-poem main count and the distinct seven-line f. 6v item, while the exact header supplies copyrighted/restricted CELT terms.

evidence.citation: Lucía Sinisi, Urbanization and Pollution in an Irish (?) Town in the 14th Century, Selim 12 (2003-2004).
evidence.locator: Discussion of sixteen versus seventeen poems and the seven-line f. 6v item
evidence.summary: Explicitly explains that the count rises from sixteen to seventeen when Five Hateful Things is included. Current SELIM pages conflict on exact CC BY-NC-ND version; no article-level version is assigned.

evidence.citation: British Library, How to reuse images of unpublished manuscripts, archived official page.
evidence.summary: Preserves the official public-domain release statement for designated unpublished-manuscript images, requested British Library shelfmark/folio credit, ethical-respect request, and request not to add new restrictions. The live link returned 404.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library copyright policy and the legacy availability notice embedded in the AJT2514 XML.
evidence.summary: The legacy notice says copyright applies where applicable and other distribution restrictions may apply. Current policy says public-domain material needs no permission, requests attribution, and leaves rights evaluation to users; neither settles all encoding-layer and contractual questions.

evidence.citation: Pinned CME repository top-level LICENSE.
evidence.summary: The Unlicense text speaks of software. It does not expressly resolve rights in imported source XML, U-M encoding, historical editorial content, or provider images.

open_questions.question: What manuscript and locus underlie Heuser's Harley 1706 appendix attribution at fol. 306, given the current 216-folio parchment description and nonmatching contents?
next_steps: Ask British Library staff to reconcile the historical shelfmark, support, extent, and foliation.
Inspect Harley MS 1706 and catalog history directly.
Do not silently substitute Harley MS 5396.

open_questions.question: What current bytes, access conditions, and component-level reuse terms apply to U-M item AJT2514.0001.001, and how do they relate to the legacy notice and raw package?
next_steps: Recheck the resolver in a normal browser or request U-M clarification without bypassing automated protections.
Seek U-M ingest/migration history and current downloadable serialization if available.
Keep current, raw, and repository rights and serialization layers separate.

open_questions.question: Can complete target images or orderable reproductions be obtained for the main and appendix witnesses whose current public selections omit the target?
next_steps: Use British Library, Bodleian, New College, CUL, and Lambeth consultation or imaging routes for the exact target loci.
Record exact image coverage and terms before adding new facsimile claims.
Retain current negative-coverage findings rather than treating public visibility of unrelated leaves as target verification.

open_questions.question: Which exact CC BY-NC-ND version, if any, applies article-by-article to the cached 2003-2004 Sinisi PDF?
next_steps: Request article-level clarification from SELIM or University of Oviedo Press.
Do not assign 3.0 Spain or 4.0 by inference while current policy pages conflict.

open_questions.question: How should Heuser's and current catalogs' differing support, date, and folio claims for Add MS 31042 and Harley MS 7358 be reconciled?
next_steps: Inspect the manuscripts or authoritative catalog histories.
Keep historical and current claims parallel until the relevant component boundaries are verified.
